title: Tragacantha vaga

taxonomy: Tragacantha vaga is a type of plant, specifically a species of shrub or small tree. It belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. The plant is also thought to have immunomodulatory properties, potentially aiding in the prevention and treatment of various infections and diseases. parts:
Tragacantha vaga is commonly known as the 'Devil's thorn' or 'devil's thorn' (Cassia absus). 

Tragacantha vaga's medicinal parts include the roots, leaves, and the seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots are used to treat fever, while the leaves are used to treat rheumatism and skin conditions. The seeds of the plant are used to treat fever and are also used as a laxative.
